title: rosa canina subsp. virens
intro: Rosa canina subsp. virens, also known as the green-leafed wild rose or dwarf wild rose, is a subspecies of the Rosa canina species, a flowering shrub native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant's hips, which are the fruit of the rose, contain high concentrations of vitamins A and C, as well as other medicinal compounds, and have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The medicinal properties of Rosa canina subsp. virens include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which contribute to its health benefits, such as reducing the risk of cardiovascular disease and improving skin health. Herbal preparations of Rosa canina subsp. virens include teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be used to support overall well-being and alleviate symptoms of certain health conditions.
